results.title: Show girls
results.creator_credit: Meilan Lam, Barrie Angus McLean, David Verrall, Tamara Lynch and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: ‘Show Girls’ celebrates Montreal’s swinging Black jazz scene from the 1920’s to the 1960’s, when the city was wide open. Three women who danced in the legendary Black clubs of the day - Rockhead’s Paradise, The Terminal, Cafe St. Michel - share their unforgettable memories of life at the center of one of the world’s hottest jazz spots. From the Roaring Twenties, through the Second World War, and on into the golden era of clubs in the fifties and sixties, ‘Show Girls’ chronicles the lives of Bernice, Tina and Olga, mixing their memories with rarely seen footage of the era. Their stories are told against a backdrop of the fascinating social and political history that made Montreal a jazz and nightclub hotspot for decades. It is a story of song and dance, music and pride. (Award: Yorkton.) Narrated by Anthony Sherwood.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Meilan Lam, Barrie Angus McLean, David Verrall, Tamara Lynch and National Film Board of Canada

results.title: Short vision
results.creator_credit: Joan Foldes, Peter Foldes and British Film Institute
results.brief_description: An experimental film: using a simple animation technique “A SHORT VISION” creates an imaginative and disturbing picture of atomic warfare. A “thing” suddenly appears in the sky, flying over countryside and cities and destroying all living things. All that finally remains is a single flame with a moth fluttering around it; and, after a moment, the flame devours the moth. Music by Matyas Seiber.

results.title: Short orders
results.creator_credit: Hal Roach, Percy Pembroke and Hal Roach Studios
results.brief_description: Stan Laurel stars in this silent film as a harried waiter who must deal with customers who have issues with the quality of the food and service in the restaurant. Various comic situations arise. Cast: Stan Laurel, Marie Mosquini, Eddie Baker, Jack Ackroyd, Mark Jones, George Rowe

results.title: Siege
results.creator_credit: Julien Bryan, Frank Donovan, Frederic Ullman, International Film Foundation and RKO Pathe Pictures
results.brief_description: Famous documentary by Julien Bryan on the invasion of Poland by the German army in September 1939 - the attack that began World War II. It captures, with powerful images, the terror and desperation of the refugees and the immense destruction in the cities and towns. Depicts the destructive effect on the lives of the people. An important work that shows how unprepared Poland was to face the German war machine. Depicts the first week of WWII, and includes quite graphic footage.

results.title: Siberia to the Silk Road
results.creator_credit: Greg Grainger, Grainger Maynard and Maynard
results.brief_description: Only weeks before the August 1991 coup in Moscow, Australian Greg Grainger embarked upon a ten thousand kilometre odyssey across the U.S.S.R., through Siberia and Central Asia, observing the lives of people at a time of extraordinary change. We observe Russia’ first ever presidential elections, visiting a polling station as Boris Yeltsin makes his historical win. A look at the lives of ordinary people in a time of extraordinary changes. See the stunning beauty of a Siberian summer, journey thousands of kilometres on the Trans Siberian Railway, drink vodka with soldiers and travel the legendary Silk Road through Uzbekistan, visiting ancient cities such as Bukhara and Samarkand.

results.title: Si Spain!
results.creator_credit: Thayer Soule and Hughes Leisure Group
results.brief_description: Filmmaker Thayer Soule takes us on a personal journey through Spain; capturing its colourful history and observing how that past has been integrated into the present. Soule travels through the quiet beauty of Castile, visits the Great Mosque in Cordoba with its 800 arches, treks through the snow-capped peaks of the Pyrenees and experiences the sights and sounds of a bullfight. The music, dancing and culture of Spain is vividly captured in this interesting personal travelogue.

results.title: Shyness
results.creator_credit: Stanley Jackson, Tom Daly and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: Abnormal shyness in children, its causes and how, through a greater understanding by parents and teachers, this problem may be dealt with. The lonely existence of a typical shy adult is portrayed, and the film then turns to a study of three children: Anna, shy but wistfully wanting association with others; Jimmy, whose excessive timidity is really a symptom of profound emotional disturbance; Robert, aloof but happily independent. Studying their conditions, a psychiatrist from the Child Guidance Clinic reveals the confidence-destroying demands of parents which predisposed the children to shyness. Together, teacher, psychiartrist and parents bring about a change in the children’s attitides. (Awards: Cleveland; New York; Toronto; Boston; Manila.)

results.title: Shrinkage
results.creator_credit: Nathan Boxer, Willard Goodman and Protective Films
results.brief_description: Shoplifting in a department store and how to deal with it. The methods used by shoplifters to distract attention and conceal stolen goods. Precautions to reduce the risk of stealing and the procedure for apprehending a person seen stealing.

results.title: Shrek [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Andrew Adamson, Vicky Jenson, Aron Warner, Jeffrey Katzenberg, John H. Williams and NBCUniversal
results.brief_description: Once upon a time in a land far, far away, and so begins this animated fantasy. Set in a fantastical kingdom inhabited by fairy tale creatures, the story finds the enchanted princess, Fiona (Cameron Diaz) locked away in a tower guarded by a dragon; a scheming Lord Farquaard (John Lithgow) who wants to marry the princess so he can become King; an unlikely hero, the lovable ogre Shrek (Mike Myers) and his sidekick, Donkey (Eddie Murphy), who are sent by Farquaard to rescue the princess and bring her back to be his bride. Children will enjoy the tale for what it is - a lively romp, while adults will enjoy the many intertextual references to numerous other fairy tales.

results.title: Showbiz ballyhoo
results.creator_credit: Gail Gibson Sedawie, Norman Sedawie, Richard Gabourie Productions and T A D Productions
results.brief_description: This documentary examines the machinations and power of the Hollywood studio publicity departments of the 1930s and 40s, a time of the studios’ heyday, when the public had an unbridled fascination for the stars of the silver screen. On screen and off, fans were sold the notion of the dream, where glamour, money, fame and beauty were in endless supply. Movie newsreels, gossip columnists and press agents played a major role in presenting actors such as Errol Flynn, Clarke Gable and Vivien Leigh as eternally glamorous and gay, spending their lives at parties and opening night galas. Actors, celebrities and child stars were also used as a propaganda tool, sending messages that supported various causes, including war bonds, bills of government and political parties. Public appearances were as important as the films that the stars made, and actors were expected to tour on junkets around the country. Footage in the documentary includes clips from iconic Hollywood films, famous screen tests, bloopers and scenes from spectacular musical productions of the 1930s. Features footage of: Humphrey Bogart, Bette Davis, Joan Crawford, Bing Crosby, James Cagney, Shirley Temple, Mickey Rooney, Judy Garland, Ronald Reagan, Rita Hayworth, Tyrone Power and The Marx Brothers.
